subject: Mozi Minute: Triple Review Merch Hack
body:
Words I like: If you can be in a bad mood for no reason, you might as well be in a good mood for no reason.

Mozi Minute: Triple Review Merch Hack

Most businesses beg for reviews or hope customers leave them naturally. Here's what actually worked for me when launching new locations: exclusive merch in exchange for triple-platform reviews.

Here's exactly what I did:

I ordered t-shirts for $5-6 each (get the nice ones). I put a cool design on it and put my logo on the back or sleeve. I asked people who wanted one for free in my online community. A ton of people did. Then I told them they were not for sale and ONLY available for people who left us an honest review on all 3 platforms (Google, Yelp, and Facebook).

The math was simple:

Cost: $5 per shirt

Return: 3 genuine reviews ($1.67 per review)

Extra benefit: Walking billboards

Key execution points:

- Make the shirts actually look good & good quality (people won't wear ugly freebies)
- Show them exactly how to leave reviews (screenshot instructions)
- Don't ask for 5-star reviews. Ask for honest reviews. Less cringe. (They'll still all be good).
- Have them show you the reviews to get the shirt
- Give the shirt immediately - no delays
- BONUS - ask them if they want you to take a picture with it on or at the facility and post it.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Make your marketing wearable,

Alex
